Member called about denied MRI claim ($1,950 member responsibility). Very frustrated — states he was never told prior auth was needed. PCP referred to ortho, ortho ordered MRI, and no one mentioned the requirement. Explained prior auth policy for advanced imaging. Member requested formal appeal. Appeal initiated, reference CASE-MB-001. Member remained upset but acknowledged the process. Call escalated to supervisor briefly for de-escalation.

Member called about Ozempic prior auth denial (AUTH-MC-001). Frustrated that her endocrinologist's recommendation was overridden by the plan's step therapy policy. Explained that the plan requires trial of Jardiance before Ozempic can be covered. Member feels this is a financial decision, not a medical one. Provided information about the step therapy process and how to get Ozempic approved after documenting Jardiance failure. Member calmed down after understanding the path forward. Moderate frustration — not hostile but clearly unhappy.

Member called about a $4,200 balance bill for anesthesia during her delivery at University Hospital. She is extremely upset — she delivered at an in-network hospital and had no say in which anesthesiologist was assigned. The hospital assigned Dr. Kowalski (out-of-network) without informing her. Member states she specifically verified the hospital was in-network before delivery. Member is aware of the No Surprises Act and believes this is a clear violation. She is emotional (first baby, already dealing with new parenthood) and feels the system failed her. Agent acknowledged the situation, confirmed this appears to qualify for surprise billing protections, and initiated an appeal. Call escalated to supervisor for expedited processing. Supervisor assured member the appeal would be prioritized. Member wants written confirmation of appeal filing.

Member called about large ER bill ($3,322 member responsibility) from hypertensive crisis on 5/14. Very concerned about the cost. Explained how the $3,000 deductible applies to facility claims and that most of the cost is the deductible being satisfied. Member requested payment plan — transferred to billing. Member was upset but understood the explanation. Not a billing error — plan processed correctly per Silver HMO benefit design.

Outbound care management enrollment call. Member has been identified for chronic condition management program due to T2D with A1C 8.5%, hypertension with recent ER visit, and multi-specialist care coordination needs. Explained program benefits: regular check-ins, medication adherence support, appointment reminders. Member was receptive and grateful for the outreach. Enrolled in program. Initial care plan discussed — member will receive monthly phone calls and quarterly care plan reviews.

Member called urgently about health insurance coverage gap. Was laid off from Meridian Healthcare Systems on 4/30 and did not initially elect COBRA. Now 20 weeks pregnant with upcoming anatomy scan and realized she has no coverage. Very anxious. Walked member through COBRA election process — still within 60-day window. Explained retroactive coverage, premium costs ($663/month), and payment timeline. Member elected COBRA on the call. Confirmed prenatal claims from May forward will be covered. Member expressed concern about COBRA cost ($663/month) — stated she can probably afford 2 months while job searching. Advised that COBRA will lapse if premiums not paid and any claims after lapse would be denied. Member understood the risk. Long call due to detailed COBRA explanation and payment setup.

Member called to add newborn baby (Isabella Rivera, DOB 10/1/2025) to her Pinnacle Financial Group Silver PPO plan. Qualifying life event — birth of child. Within 30-day enrollment window. Processed dependent enrollment effective retroactive to 10/1/2025. Coverage type changed from Individual to Family. Member asked about well-baby visits and pediatric coverage — confirmed preventive care is 100% covered. Member happy and appreciative. Straightforward enrollment call.
